metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: Kenya’s First IPO in 11 Years Scrapes Through With Uganda’s Help
all.description: The initial public offering of Kenya’s state-run pipeline company, the first such sale for the East African nation in more than a decade, exceeded its target by 5.7%

all.headline: Oil Pares Gains, Markets Steady After Report of Iran Outreach
This Year’s Winning Stock Trades Take the Brunt of War Selloff
all.description: Asian stocks are plunging, in part because the region’s economies depend on imported oil.

all.headline: Myanmar to Curb Private Vehicle Use as Fuel Shortages Emerge
all.description: Myanmar’s military government will impose limits on private vehicles starting Saturday to conserve fuel as disruptions to Middle East shipping routes threaten the country’s energy supplies.

all.headline: Ghana Inflation Boosts Rate-Cut Hopes as War Clouds Outlook
all.description: Ghana’s inflation rate fell to a near three-decade low in February, bolstering expectations for a fifth straight rate cut even as conflict in the Middle East threatens to rekindle global price pressur

all.headline: EDP CEO: Energy Shock From War Fueling Clean Power Demand
Euro-Zone Unemployment Unexpectedly Declines to Record Low
all.description: Unemployment in the euro area unexpectedly dropped to a record low as economic expansion outpaces forecasts.

all.headline: Stocks Rise, Dollar Slides on Iran Contact Report: Markets Wrap
all.description: Stocks rebounded after a report that Iran made indirect contact with the US to negotiate an end to the war in the Middle East, boosting hopes that the conflict could be short-lived. The dollar halted

all.headline: S. Africa Treasury: Can Weather Shocks Like Iran War
all.description: South Africa says it's built a buffer strong enough to withstand global shocks like the Iran war. Treasury officials say it would take a very large shock to derail the country's fiscal plans. Chief Af

all.headline: European Gas Slips on Report Iran Is Ready to Discuss Ending War
all.description: European natural gas halted its biggest rally in four years after a report that Iran is ready to discuss ending the conflict that has rattled energy markets.

all.headline: UK stock market calms but oil prices rise over fears Iran war may drag on
all.description: UK and other European markets opened a little higher but gas and crude prices remain volatile.

all.headline: Use our tax calculator to see how Spring Statement forecasts may affect you
all.description: Wages are forecast to go up over the next five years but you could pay more tax because of frozen thresholds.

all.headline: No new tax rises in Spring Statement, but don't be fooled - tax bills are still rising
all.description: There are measures, announced ahead of the chancellor's Spring Statement, yet to take effect.

all.headline: What Spring Statement forecasts could mean for your money
all.description: Among the data and projections are key figures that shine a light on prospects for your finances.

all.headline: Trump says US Navy will protect ships in Middle East 'if necessary'
all.description: The US President said he was taking steps to keep energy supplies flowing as oil and gas prices continued to surge.

all.headline: What are my rights if my flight is cancelled or delayed?
all.description: We look at the different circumstances that affect you if you're due a refund for cancelled or delayed flights.

all.headline: Trump threatens to halt trade with Spain over military base access
all.description: Trump lashed out after Spain barred the US from using its military bases to carry out strikes on Iran.

all.headline: Gas and oil prices soar and shares tumble on fears conflict could escalate
all.description: Markets react as the conflict in the Middle East intensifies and concerns grow over how long it will last.

all.headline: 'I make between £800 and £2,000 a month on zero-hours contracts. There's no consistency'
all.description: People in their 20s and 30s tell the BBC how they feel about their finances as the chancellor delivers her Spring Statement.

all.headline: OpenAI changes deal with US military after backlash
all.description: Chief Executive Sam Altman said the group would prohibit the use of its systems to spy on Americans.

all.headline: Spain's Pedro Sánchez hits back at Trump threat to sever trade saying 'no to war'
all.description: Spain's prime minister delivers a strong rebuttal to US President Donald Trump's threat to end trade with his country.

all.headline: US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking
all.description: Washington says the two countries are combatting what the US calls designated terrorist organisations in the South American country.

all.headline: What to know about the 'two sessions', China's biggest political meeting
all.description: The carefully choreographed annual meetings signal the priorities of the world's second-largest economy.

all.headline: TikTok won't protect DMs with controversial privacy tech, saying it would put users at risk
all.description: TikTok tells the BBC it won't join rival platforms such as WhatsApp and Messenger in using end-to-end encryption.

all.headline: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder of newborn baby
all.description: The case has gripped South Korea, where there is no regulation on how far into pregnancy an abortion can take place.

all.headline: South Africa's long-serving former defence minister and former Robben Island prisoner dies
all.description: Mosiuoa Lekota was imprisoned with former South African President Nelson Mandela on Robben Island.

all.headline: Guilty verdict for US father of teenage school shooter
all.description: Prosecutors say Colin Gray, 55, could have prevented his son from killing four people at a high school in Georgia in 2024.

all.headline: Australian girl, 8, killed in snowmobile accident in Japan
all.description: It is the fourth Australian death at a Japanese ski resort this year.

all.headline: US commerce secretary to testify before Congress about Epstein ties
all.description: The cabinet member "voluntarily" agreed to be interviewed, weeks after acknowledging that he visited Epstein's island.

all.headline: Here’s how Londoners will try to stop China’s massive embassy
all.description: A band of nearby homeowners tests whether 'the mighty Chinese Communist Party is any match for U.K. planning law.'

all.headline: Keep calm and carry on: Britain’s finance minister tries to dodge the Biden trap
all.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves is trying not to tell British voters they’ll feel rich too hastily. Time is not on her side — and global turmoil is setting her back further
